text:
Market today: Ugly week for tech. AI momentum cracked hard - Oracle's worst week since 2001, Micron down 6%, software names whipsawing. The real driver is a double hit: AI financing concerns spooking the chip/data-center trade, plus a U.S. strike on Iran pulling oil up and adding macro risk. Your names live at the center of both.

Your holdings: AVGO is the one screaming for attention - your largest position, down 16.36%, and the AI infrastructure selloff is its direct headwind. PLTR is off 13.21% with no obvious near-term catalyst to reverse it. SPAL is down 16.92% - remember this is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily; on margin that's a dangerous combination. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile, limited price history - treat it as a live grenade.

Watch: (1) Whether AI capex/financing fears keep spreading - that's what kills AVGO and SMH. (2) Oil price reaction to the Iran strike opening bell - macro shock can accelerate your drawdowns fast on margin.

Bottom line: You have three positions bleeding double digits on margin - size, stops, and cash buffer matter more than any new trade right now.
